title: Fragrant Egyptian Eggplant Stew
description: A fragrant, herb‑laden eggplant stew that captures the romantic flavors of Egypt, simmered slowly with tomatoes, garlic, and a touch of cumin for a light, Mediterranean‑friendly dish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large heavy‑bottom pot over medium‑high heat.
Add the eggplant cubes and brown them on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the diced onion and cook until translucent, 3–4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute to caramelize the flavors.
Pour in the tomato sauce and broth, stir well, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat, add cumin, salt, and pepper, then cover and let the stew simmer for 25–30 minutes, until the eggplant is tender.
Stir in the chopped parsley and, if using, the lemon juice; adjust seasoning with extra salt or pepper.
Serve the stew hot over a bed of fluffy rice, couscous, or with warm flatbread.
